frameworks.name: orientation tuning / functional cortical maps
frameworks.one_line: V1 neurons selective for stimulus orientation organized into cellular maps.

frameworks.name: end-stopping / length tuning
frameworks.one_line: Receptive-field length suppression characterized across cortical depth.

frameworks.name: two-photon calcium imaging at single-neuron resolution
frameworks.one_line: Population-scale imaging in awake macaque cortex over long durations.

core_question: What are the microstructural and population orientation-tuning properties of awake macaque V1 superficial layers revealed by long-term two-photon calcium imaging?
key_assumptions: Calcium signal amplitude reflects orientation-selective spiking response
Awake macaque V1 superficial layers are accessible to chronic 2P imaging
Orientation preference and bandwidth are stable enough for cellular mapping
manipulations: Stimulus length (for end-stopping / length tuning estimation)
key_findings: Cellular orientation maps show horizontal and vertical clustering by preference, not by bandwidth
Pinwheel centers occurred less frequently than previous estimates
Orientation tuning bandwidths were narrower than prior layer-specific single-unit estimates
Cardinal and oblique orientations were similarly represented in count and bandwidth; more end-stopped cells at 150 micron depth without bandwidth change
interpretations: V1 orientation selectivity is more precise than prior recordings suggested
Minimal V1 representation of the oblique effect; end-stopping varies with depth independent of orientation bandwidth
limitations_noted: Length-tuning estimates are described as rough
